summary: A two-story residential building with a gable roof. The structure features wood plank walls on both floors with alternating stonebrick bands between levels. Multiple glass windows are distributed across both floors. The building sits on a stonebrick foundation and is surrounded by a dirt courtyard.

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: foundation, bands between floors, roof trim

rebuild_hints: First floor: wood plank walls with 4 evenly-spaced glass windows
Add horizontal stonebrick band at floor 2 level
Second floor: wood plank walls with 4 evenly-spaced glass windows
Add wooden fence posts at all four corners
Construct gable roof using dark wood/nether brick blocks, sloped on both sides
Roof peak appears to be 1-2 blocks above the second floor walls
Surround building with dirt/coarse dirt courtyard
uncertainties: Exact window placement pattern (appears symmetric but precise spacing unclear)
Interior layout and room divisions not visible
Exact roof peak height and slope angle
Whether corner posts are fence or wood logs
Exact shade of roof material (appears very dark, possibly nether brick or dark oak)
